response: "Substituting x=2 into f(x)=2x^3-3x^2+ax+b yields 16-12+2a+b=0, which simplifies to the relationship 2a+b=-4. For x=2 to be a double root, the derivative f'(x)=6x^2-6x+a must also equal zero at x=2. Evaluating f'(2) gives 24-12+a=0, so a=-12. Plugging a=-12 into 2a+b=-4 results in -24+b=-4, meaning b=20. Thus a equals -12 and b equals 20."
